title: Helianthopsis pseudoverbesinoides
taxonomy: Helianthopsis pseudoverbesinoides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Helianthopsis. Further classification places it within the order Lamiales. The family Scrophulariaceae is a group of flowering plants that includes some 300 species. Helianthopsis pseudoverbesinoides is native to tropical Africa.

intro: Helianthopsis pseudoverbesinoides is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Helianthopsis pseudoverbesinoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tea and infusions, which are traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
